overview.summary: This module owns the product canonical — the master reference record for every financial instrument in NMD. It is the authoritative identity layer that all ingestion, normalization, and export modules resolve their data against.
overview.purpose: To define the functional requirements for maintaining and querying the NMD product canonical entity table, including the 5-column hierarchical model, canonical key computation, status lifecycle, and cross-module query interface.
overview.audience: Developers, QA engineers, and business analysts building or testing the NMD platform

in_scope: Product canonical entity table maintenance — create, update, deactivate records
5-column hierarchical model: Exchange, Commodity, Product Type, Product Code, Contract
Status lifecycle management: Active, Inactive, Disable with top-down inheritance
User and Source Processing creation and status management
Full audit trail for all canonical record changes
Canonical query interface — lookup by key, source identifier, and attributes
Phase 1 asset classes: Futures & Options, FX
out_of_scope: Data ingestion source management and processing (FRD-NMD-0002)
Query and export APIs for downstream systems (FRD-NMD-0003)
Phase 2+ asset classes: Equities, Fixed Income, Structured Products

items.definition: A single row in the canonical entity table representing one unique financial instrument, identified by its canonical key
A deterministic hash computed from all 5 hierarchy columns (with null placeholders for skipped levels) that uniquely identifies a market data entity across all NMD modules
The structural model: Exchange (L1) → Commodity (L2) → Product [Type + Code] (L3) → Contract (L4)
A category of financial instruments (e.g., Futures & Options, FX) that defines its own mapping of the 5 hierarchy columns and supported product types
A designated data source whose ingested data is treated as authoritative for canonical record creation and update, as defined in the source configuration
The automatic propagation of a canonical record's status change to all descendant records in the hierarchy, exempting records already in Disabled state
The automated ingestion pipeline (FRD-NMD-0002) that creates or updates canonical records based on source configuration

business_context.content: The product canonical is the data foundation of NMD. Without a clean, maintained canonical, cross-source data linkage is impossible — every ingestion run, query, and export depends on resolving instrument identity against this table. This module eliminates the absence of a single authoritative product record that currently causes data inconsistency across internal systems.

items.name: Data Owner
items.role: Creates, updates, and manages canonical records and status via the Operator UI

items.name: Operator UI
items.role: Exposes canonical management and query functions to data owners

items.name: Data Ingestion Module
items.role: Resolves ingested data against canonical records; auto-creates canonical records when processing data from a golden source

items.name: Query & Export Module
items.role: Reads canonical records to enrich and serve downstream queries and exports

functional_overview.content: The Product Canonical module provides two core capabilities: (1) Canonical Management — full lifecycle management of instrument master records including creation, update, deactivation, status management, and audit trail; (2) Canonical Query — a read interface for all internal modules to resolve instrument identity by canonical key, source identifier, or attribute search.

items.trigger: Data owner creates a new instrument record via UI
Data owner updates an existing canonical record
Data owner deactivates or retires an instrument
Data owner changes the status of a canonical record
Internal module requests canonical lookup by source identifier
Internal module requests canonical lookup by canonical key
Data owner searches canonical by attribute (exchange, commodity, product type, product code)
Data Ingestion Module processes data from a golden source and encounters an instrument

requirements.description: System shall maintain a canonical entity table with 5 columns representing 4 hierarchical layers: Exchange (L1), Commodity (L2), Product Type + Product Code (L3 — Product), and Contract (L4)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Canonical table exists with all 5 columns; all Phase 1 asset classes can be represented

requirements.description: System shall compute a deterministic canonical key as a hash of all 5 columns in fixed order, using null placeholders for skipped levels; the same 5-column input must always produce the same key
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Hash is reproducible across all callers; same 5-column values always produce identical key

requirements.description: The 4-layer structure shall be defined as: Exchange (primary trading venue), Commodity (underlying of the product), Product (type + code — the product published/traded on the exchange), Contract (additional contract identifier for derivatives)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All 4 layers are present and correctly defined in the data model

requirements.description: Each asset class shall define its own mapping of the 5 columns and its supported product types; no asset class may reuse another's column mapping or product type definitions
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 asset classes (Futures & Options, FX) each have distinct, non-overlapping column mappings and product type definitions

requirements.description: System shall support three canonical status values: Active (all reference data and daily updates imported), Inactive (reference data only), Disable (no data loaded)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All three status values are supported; ingestion pipeline gates data loading by canonical status correctly

requirements.description: Canonical status shall be inherited top-down — a status change at a parent level shall trigger automatic status sync to all descendant records, except descendants already in Disabled state which remain unchanged
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Parent status change cascades correctly to all non-Disabled descendants; Disabled descendants are unaffected

requirements.description: Canonical status shall be manageable by: User (may set any record to any status, subject to parent constraints), or Source Processing (status defined by source configuration)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Both User and Source Processing can set canonical status within defined rules

requirements.description: Canonical records shall be created by: User (manual creation via UI — bulk import not required in Phase 1), or Source Processing (auto-created as defined by source processing configuration)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Both creation paths produce valid canonical records with correct initial status

requirements.description: System shall maintain a full audit trail of all canonical record changes — capturing who or what made the change, when, which field changed, old value, and new value
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Every canonical write produces an immutable audit log entry; no write path bypasses the audit trail

requirements.description: System shall support lookup of a canonical record by canonical key (hash)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Exact match by canonical key returns correct record or NOT_FOUND

requirements.description: System shall support lookup by source-specific identifier, returning the matching canonical record
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Source identifier resolves to correct canonical record across all Phase 1 asset classes

requirements.description: System shall support search by one or more attributes: Exchange, Commodity, Product Type, Product Code
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Attribute search returns all matching records; supports single and multi-attribute filter combinations

requirements.description: System shall return the full canonical record including all 5 hierarchy columns, canonical key, status, and audit metadata
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All required fields present in every query response

requirements.description: System shall return only Active canonical records by default; support optional filter to include Inactive and Disabled records
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Default query excludes Inactive and Disabled; optional filter correctly includes them

items.rule: Exchange is mandatory on every canonical record — no record may exist without an Exchange
Canonical key is a deterministic hash of all 5 columns in fixed order; null placeholders used for skipped levels; same 5-column values always produce the same key
Each asset class defines its own mapping of the 5 columns AND its supported product types; no asset class may reuse another's column mapping or product type definitions

Status cascade is top-down: Exchange → Commodity → Product → Contract; a parent status change propagates to all descendants except children already in Disabled state — Disabled children are exempt from cascade and remain Disabled
A child record's status may not be more permissive than its parent: child cannot be Active if parent is Inactive or Disabled; child cannot be Inactive if parent is Disabled
User may set any record to any status directly (subject to BRL-005); Source Processing may only set status as defined in its source configuration
When Source Processing creates a canonical record, initial status is defined by the source configuration — not defaulted to Active
Duplicate canonical records are not permitted — identical 5-column hash cannot be inserted twice
Audit trail entries are immutable — no audit record may be updated or deleted after creation

items.trigger: Source Processing attempts to create a canonical record with a hash that already exists
items.expected_behaviour: Reject creation; return conflict error with existing record reference; do not update existing record

items.trigger: User attempts to set a child record to Active when parent is Inactive or Disabled
items.expected_behaviour: Reject; return validation error stating parent status constraint

items.trigger: User attempts to set a child record to Inactive when parent is Disabled
items.expected_behaviour: Reject; return validation error stating parent status constraint

items.trigger: Parent status change cascade encounters DB error mid-update
items.expected_behaviour: Roll back entire cascade; leave all records at previous status; alert operator. Note: children already in Disabled state not changing is expected behaviour — not a failure

items.trigger: Source Processing attempts to create a canonical record missing Exchange
items.expected_behaviour: Reject; return validation error; log failed attempt with source details

items.trigger: Canonical lookup by source-specific identifier returns no match
items.expected_behaviour: Return empty result with NOT_FOUND status code; do not auto-create

items.trigger: Hash collision — two distinct 5-column combinations produce the same hash
items.expected_behaviour: Log critical alert; block both records until resolved; never silently overwrite

events_published.trigger: New canonical record created by User or Source Processing
Status changed on any record including cascade-triggered changes

data_owned.notes: Single authoritative table — all other modules are read-only consumers
Immutable; owned and written only by this module

items.purpose: Provides source configuration — golden source flag, initial status on creation, types of ingestion feed
Delivers user actions for canonical CRUD and status changes

entities.name: Canonical Record
entities.notes: No hard delete — retirement via Disable status only

entities.name: Canonical Audit Log
entities.notes: Immutable — no update or delete permitted

data_requirements.data_retention: Indefinite — canonical records and audit log are permanent; no purge policy

items.description: Canonical query response time shall meet the platform latency target (TBD, e.g., <100ms p95) — this is a hot path called by every ingestion run
Status cascade on a large parent (e.g., disabling an Exchange with thousands of descendants) shall complete within a defined SLA window without blocking canonical queries
Canonical record writes and status changes shall be transactional — partial updates are not permitted
Canonical data is the cross-module golden source — read access available to all internal modules; write access restricted to authorised users and Source Processing only
All canonical data at rest shall be encrypted
All canonical writes shall be captured in the audit log — no write path may bypass the audit trail

items.criterion: Canonical entity table supports all 5 columns across all Phase 1 asset classes (Futures & Options, FX) with correct column mappings
Canonical key hash is deterministic — same 5-column input always produces the same key across all callers
Status cascade correctly propagates to all descendants on parent status change; Disabled children remain unchanged
User status constraint violations (EX-002a, EX-002b) are rejected with correct error responses
All canonical writes produce an immutable audit log entry with full field-level change detail
Canonical query API returns correct results for lookup by key, lookup by source identifier, and attribute search
Canonical query returns Active records only by default; Inactive and Disabled records returned only when explicitly requested
Source Processing auto-creation applies initial status from source configuration — never defaults to Active
Duplicate canonical records (same hash) are rejected by both User and Source Processing creation paths
All canonical data at rest is encrypted; write access restricted to authorised users and Source Processing

assumptions.description: Asset class column mappings for Futures & Options and FX will be defined before detailed design begins
Hash algorithm selection (e.g., SHA-256, MD5, MurmurHash) will be agreed upon before implementation — all modules must use the same algorithm
Source Processing (FRD-NMD-0002) will define and own the golden source flag and initial status configuration — this module consumes that configuration
No bulk import is required for Phase 1 — data owners create canonical records manually or via Source Processing

constraints.description: Phase 1 asset classes: Futures & Options and FX only
No hard deletes — canonical records are permanent; retirement via Disable status only
Write access to canonical table restricted to this module only — all other modules are read-only consumers

items.description: FRD-NMD-0002 (Source Processing) — must define source configuration schema (golden source flag, initial status, ingestion feed types) before canonical module design is finalised
Asset class mapping definitions for Phase 1 (Futures & Options, FX) — required before canonical table schema can be finalised
Hash algorithm agreed across all NMD modules — canonical key computation must be consistent platform-wide

items.issue: Hash algorithm not yet selected — impacts canonical key computation across all modules
Asset class column mappings for Phase 1 not yet defined — impacts table schema and validation rules
Specific exchange(s) for Futures & Options Phase 1 not yet confirmed — impacts asset class mapping
